meta.description: Complete knowledge of Deriv synthetic indices: behaviour, character, best timeframes, and how to analyse each family.

overview.whatTheyAre: Synthetic indices simulated by a cryptographically secure random number generator, audited, running 24/7 with constant defined volatility. No news, no sessions, no gaps (except Boom/Crash spikes).
advantages: Constant statistical volatility -> ATR and probability models are stable.
No fundamental news shocks -> pure technical analysis works cleanly.
implications: Because volatility is engineered, indicator settings stay consistent per symbol.
Support/resistance and structure still form from order flow of the pricing engine and trader behaviour on entries/exits.
Backtests are more representative than on news-driven markets.

R_10.name: Volatility 10
R_10.character: Slow, smooth, small candles. Good for beginners and tight-stop scalps.

R_75.name: Volatility 75
R_75.character: Fast, strong impulses, deep pullbacks. Most popular for scalping. Needs wider stops.

R_100.name: Volatility 100
R_100.character: Very fast and volatile. Big candles; strict risk control mandatory.

volatility.oneSecondVariants: 1s versions (e.g. 1HZ75V) tick every second instead of every 2 seconds: faster charts, same volatility profile.
howToAnalyse: Treat like a normal market: full MTF analysis, structure, patterns, indicators all valid.
Match stop distance to the symbol's ATR — V75/V100 need 2-3x wider stops than V10/V25.
Trend days are common: ADX > 25 means ride pullbacks, do not fade.

boomCrash.character: Boom drifts DOWN with sudden UP spikes. Crash drifts UP with sudden DOWN spikes. 300 series spikes most often, 1000 least often.
howToAnalyse: Two separate games: spike catching vs drift riding — never mix them in one position.
Always know ticks since last spike before any trade (see spike-detection.json).
Zones matter: spikes frequently launch from HTF demand (Boom) or supply (Crash).
M1 for execution, M5/M15 for zones, H1 for macro drift channel.
hardRules: On Boom: never hold sells into an overdue spike window.
On Crash: never hold buys into an overdue spike window.

step.character: Step Index moves in fixed-size steps with equal up/down probability. Extremely clean structure, tight ranges.
howToAnalyse: Best for pure structure and S/R trading; levels are respected cleanly.
Range strategies excel; use ADX to detect the rare trend phases.

jump.character: Uniform volatility with jumps (about 3 per hour on average) that move price ~30x normal in either direction.
howToAnalyse: Like volatility indices but with random jump risk both directions.
Wider stops or smaller size to survive jumps; jumps also create breakout opportunities.
Never place stops just beyond obvious levels — jumps hunt them.

rangeBreak.character: Range break indices oscillate in a range then break out after a set average number of attempts.
howToAnalyse: Trade the range edges until a statistical breakout window, then switch to breakout mode.

R_75.strategy: EMA 9/21 pullback scalps in M15 trend direction; SL 1.5xATR(14); TP 2xSL. Avoid entries right after a 3xATR impulse candle (wait for pullback).
R_75.notes: Respects fib 0.5-0.618 golden zone well on M5 legs.

R_100.strategy: Breakout-retest of M15 ranges with volume confirmation. Too fast for M1 beginners.

R_10.strategy: Mean reversion off Bollinger extremes in ranges; tiny targets, high win-rate style.
R_10.notes: Small ATR means small absolute profit — size accordingly, never over-leverage to compensate.

BOOM1000.strategy: Buy at HTF demand with spike probability >= 65, or sell fresh drift immediately post-spike.
BOOM1000.notes: Longest cycle: drift sells have the most room, but discipline on exit is critical.

CRASH1000.strategy: Sell at HTF supply with spike probability >= 65, or buy fresh drift immediately post-spike.

BOOM500_CRASH500.strategy: Same logic as 1000 series but cycles are twice as fast: reduce hold time and targets accordingly.

commonMistakes: Treating Boom/Crash like normal markets and shorting Boom randomly (spike wipes the account).
Using the same stop distance across V10 and V75.
Overtrading 1s indices due to the speed of candles.
Ignoring that synthetics run 24/7 — fatigue leads to revenge trading; use session limits from risk-management.json.
